title: Compositions, systems, and methods for sensing
abstract: Gel compositions that enable sensing of signals in a subject, systems comprising the same, and methods of use thereof are described. The gel compositions may receive and transmit an input signal (such as an electrical signal) and convert the input signal into an output signal. The gel compositions may be integrated into washable sensors, which may be integrated into wearable smart products that permit the output of a report indicative of the health or physiological parameter(s) or condition(s) of a subject when the washable sensor makes physical contact with the subject.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A composition comprising a gel, wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with hexane such that a pH of said hexane changes by no more than 2 pH units in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C; wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with water such that a pH of said water changes by no more than 2 pH units in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C; and wherein said gel is not a hydrogel. 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with said hexane such that said pH of said hexane changes by no more than 1 pH unit in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C; wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with said water such that said pH of said water changes by no more than 1 pH unit in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C. 3. The composition of claim 1 or 2, wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with said hexane such that said pH of said hexane changes by no more than 0.5 pH unit in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C; wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with said water such that said pH of said water changes by no more than 0.5 pH unit in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C. 4. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with said hexane such that said pH of said hexane changes by no more than 0.1 pH unit in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C; wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with said water such that said pH of said water changes by no more than 0.1 pH unit in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C. 5. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with said hexane such that said pH of said hexane changes by no more than 2 pH units in a period of at least 2 days at a temperature of 25 Â°C; wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with said water such that said pH of said water changes by no more than 2 pH units in a period of at least 2 days at a temperature of 25 Â°C. 6. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein said gel is configured to contact a subject and sense one or more signals from said subject, wherein said one or more signals correspond to a charge, voltage, resistance, conductance, current, or impedance, or a change thereof. 7. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein said gel is configured to contact a subject and be actuated to provide one or more output signals to said subject, wherein said one or more output signals correspond to a force, pressure, charge, voltage, resistance, conductance, current, or impedance, or a change thereof. 8. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein said gel has an electrical impedance of no more than 2 times higher than a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both, as measured by an Electrochemical Impedance Spectrometer (EIS) at a frequency of 0.1 Hz. 9. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein said gel has an electrical impedance of no more than 2 times higher than a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both, after 5 wash cycles, or after 10 wash cycles, or after 20 wash cycles, or after 50 wash cycles as measured by an EIS at a frequency of 0.1 Hz. 10. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein said gel has an electrical impedance of no more than 5 times higher than a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both, after 5 wash cycles, or after 10 wash cycles, or after 20 wash cycles, or after 50 wash cycles as measured by an EIS at a frequency of 0.1 Hz. 11. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein said gel has an electrical impedance of at least 2% lower, at least 5% lower, or at least 10% lower than a reference wet- electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both, after 5 wash cycles, or after 10 wash cycles, or after 20 wash cycles, or after 50 wash cycles as measured by an EIS at a frequency of 0.1 Hz. 12. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein said gel has an electrical impedance of no more than 10 times higher than a reference wet-electrode after 50 wash cycles as measured by an EIS at a frequency of 0.1 Hz. 13. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein said gel has a Youngâs modulus of about 0.05 megapascal (MPa) to about 0.15 MPa as measured by an instron testing machine. 14. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein said gel has a Youngâs modulus of about 0.05 MPa to about 0.15 MPa after 5 wash cycles as measured by an instron testing machine. 15. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein said gel has a Youngâs modulus of about 0.05 MPa to about 0.15 MPa after 20 wash cycles as measured by an instron testing machine. 16. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein said gel has a breaking elongation of about 50% to about 300% as measured by an instron testing machine. 17. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein said gel has a breaking elongation of at least 90% as measured by an instron testing machine. 18. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein said gel has a surface tackiness of about 1 kilogram per square meter (kg/m 2 ) to about 10,000 kg/m 2 as measured by a shear rheometer. 19. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein said gel has a surface tackiness of about 1 kg/m 2 to about 10,000 kg/m 2 after 5 wash cycles as measured by a shear rheometer. 20. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein said gel has a surface tackiness of about 1 kg/m 2 to about 10,000 kg/m 2 after 20 wash cycles as measured by a shear rheometer. 21. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein said gel has a thickness of about 0.01 millimeter to about 50 millimeters (mm). 22. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 21, wherein said gel weighs at least 80% of its initial weight after 5 wash cycles. 23. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 22, wherein said gel weighs at least 80% of its initial weight after 10 wash cycles. 24. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 23, wherein said gel weighs at least 80% of its initial weight after 20 wash cycles. 25. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein said gel is formed by curing from a mixture comprising an ionic salt, monomers, an initiator, a crosslinker, and optionally a catalyst. 26. The composition of claim 25, wherein said monomers each comprise a vinyl or allyl group; and said monomers are selected from the group consisting of acrylamide (AAm), methacrylic acid (MAA), methyl methacrylate (MMA), acrylic acid (AA), butyl acrylate (BA), dimethylacrylamide (DMAAm), methyl ether acrylate (MPEGDA), and any combination thereof. 27. The composition of claim 25 or 26, wherein said initiator comprises a thermochemical initiator selected from the group consisting of a disulfide-based initiator, a peroxide-based initiator, a persulfate-based initiator, a ketone-based initiator, and a combination thereof. 28. The composition of any one of claims 25 to 27, wherein said crosslinker comprises a divinyl terminated reagent. 29. The composition of any one of claims 25 to 28, wherein said mixture comprises a humectant. 30. The composition of claim 29, wherein said humectant is selected from the group consisting of a cellulose derivative, a polyamine-containing reagent, and a polycarboxy- containing reagent. 31. The composition of any one of claims 25 to 30, wherein said ionic salt is selected from the group consisting of a cationic polymer, a conductive polymer, carbon black, carbon nanotube, a carbon fiber, a metal plated fiber, an ionic salt, and an ionic polymer. 32. The composition of claim 31, wherein said conductive polymer is selected from the group consisting of poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) polystyrene sulfonate (PEDOT:PSS), polyaniline (PANI), and polythiophene (PT). 33. The composition of claim 31 or 32, wherein said ionic salt or polymer comprises a 1 -butyl-3 -methyl imidazolium (BMIM) salt with presence of counter ion X (BMIM-X), wherein said X is selected from the group consisting of [BF4], [Tf2N], [PF6], [TFSI], and [OctS04] 34. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 33, wherein said ionic salt is configured to enhance a conductivity of said gel. 35. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 34, wherein said gel comprises a polymeric ionic gel derived from a mixture comprising by weight: about 10% to about 90% monomers; about 10% to about 90% a crosslinker; about 0.01% to about 20% an initiator; and about 1% to about 50% an ionic salt. 36. The composition of claim 35, wherein: said monomers comprise butyl acrylate (BA); said crosslinker comprises polyethylene glycol diacrylate (PEGDA), polyethylene glycol) methyl ether acrylate (MPEGDA), or a combination thereof; said initiator comprises 1 -hydroxy cyclohexyl phenyl ketone (photo initiator 184); and said ionic salt comprises a 1 -butyl-3 -methyl imidazolium (BMIM) salt with presence of counter ion X (BMIM-X), wherein said X is selected from the group consisting of [BF4], [Tf2N], [PF6], [TFSI], and [OctS04] 37. The composition of claim 36, wherein said mixture comprises by weight: about 0.1% to about 0.5% PEGDA, and about 35% to about 50% MPEGDA. 38. The composition of any one of claims 25 to 37, wherein said mixture has a viscosity of between 0.001 centipoise (cP) and 100,000 centipoises (cP) as measured by a viscometer. 39. The composition of any one of claims 25 to 38, wherein said mixture is conformally applied by printing on a flexible or deformable substrate. 40. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 39, wherein said gel is configured to conformably rest upon a flexible or deformable substrate. 41. The composition of claim 39 or 40, wherein said flexible or deformable substrate is selected from the group consisting of a textile, a fiber, a yarn, a fabric, a film, paper, a plastic, and an elastomer. 42. The composition of any one of claims 39 to 41, wherein said gel is configured to transmit a sensed signal to an electrode or interconnect placed on said flexible or deformable substrate; and wherein said subject is stationary or in motion. 43. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 42, wherein said gel is configured to conformally adhere to skin of said subject. 44. The composition of claim 43, wherein said gel is configured to result in a skin-to- electrode impedance of about 0.5 MW to about 3.0 MW as measured by an Electrochemical Impedance Spectrometer (EIS) at a frequency of 0.1 Hz; and wherein said subject is stationary or in motion. 45. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 44, wherein said one or more signals are associated with a vital sign of said subject, selected from the group consisting of skin impedance, galvanic skin response, hydration level, blood glucose level, pH level, heart-rate, heart-rate variability, electrocardiogram (ECG) signal, electroencephalogram (EEG) signal, and electromyogram (EMG) signal. 46. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 45, wherein said gel is configured to provide actuation for said subject upon receiving an input signal, wherein said actuation is transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (TENS), or electrical muscle stimulation (EMS), or a combination thereof. 47. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 46, wherein said gel is configured to reduce motion artifacts during sensing by providing a clearer waveform under various motions compared to a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both. 48. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 47, wherein said gel is configured to reduce motion artifacts during sensing by reducing at least 10% baseline noise compared to a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both. 49. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 48, wherein said gel is configured to improve accuracy of sensing by at least 10% compared to a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both. 50. The composition of any one of claims 1 to 49, wherein said gel is a reusable, inherently adhesive ionically conductive polymer gel system. 51. A device comprising a sensor, which sensor comprises: (i) at least one electrode or interconnect, and (ii) a gel adjacent to said at least one electrode or interconnect, wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with hexane such that a pH of said hexane changes by no more than 2 pH units in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C; wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with water such that a pH of said water changes by no more than 2 pH units in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C; and wherein said gel is not a hydrogel. 52. The device of claim 51, further comprising a flexible or deformable substrate adjacent to said at least one electrode or interconnect. 53. The device of claim 51 or 52, further comprising a protective gel layer on top of at least a part of said at least one electrode or interconnect. 54. The device of claim 53, wherein said protective gel layer comprises silicone. 55. The device of any one of claims 51 to 54, wherein said gel is configured to withstand up to 100 machine washes and drying cycles, or 200 hand washes and air drying cycles. 56. The device of any one of claims 51 to 55, wherein said device is an intimate garment. 57. The device of any one of claims 51 to 55, wherein said device is a compression textile. 58. The device of any one of claims 51 to 55, wherein said device is an elastic band. 59. The device of any one of claims 51 to 55, wherein said device is a skin patch for one-time use, long-term use, or for multiple uses. 60. The device of any one of claims 51 to 59, wherein said device is reusable after at least 5, at least 10, or at least 20 cycles of washing. 61. The device of any one of claims 51 to 60, wherein said device is configured to withstand up to 100 machine washes and drying cycles, or 200 hand washes and air drying cycles. 62. The device of any one of claims 51 to 61, wherein said device is wearable, or implantable. 63. A method for sensing one or more signals from a subject, comprising: (a) providing a sensor comprising (i) at least one electrode and (ii) a gel adjacent to said at least one electrode, wherein said gel is configured to come in contact 

 with hexane such that a pH of said hexane changes by no more than 2 pH units in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C; wherein said gel is configured to come in contact with water such that a pH of said water changes by no more than 2 pH units in a period of at least 1 day at a temperature of 25 Â°C; and wherein said gel rests adjacent to a body of said subject; (b) using said at least one electrode to measure said one or more signals corresponding to a health or physiological parameter or condition of said subject; and (c) using said one or more signals to output a report indicative of said health or physiological parameter or condition of said subject. 64. The method of claim 63, wherein said one or more signals correspond to a charge, voltage, resistance, conductance, current, or impedance, or a change thereof, of said body of said subject. 65. The method of claim 63 or 64, wherein said health or physiological parameter or condition of said subject is selected from the group consisting of skin impedance, hydration level, blood glucose level, pH level, heart-rate, heart-rate variability, electrocardiogram (ECG) signal, electroencephalogram (EEG) signal, and electromyogram (EMG) signal. 66. The method of any one of claims 63 to 65, wherein said subject is stationary or in motion. 67. The method of any one of claims 63 to 66, wherein said gel, upon contacting said body of said subject, conformally adhere to said body of said subject. 68. The method of any one of claims 63 to 67, wherein said gel, upon contacting said body of said subject, result in a skin-to-electrode electrical impedance of about 0.5 MW to about 6.5 MW as measured by an Electrochemical Impedance Spectrometer (EIS) at a frequency of 0.1 Hz. 69. The method of any one of claims 63 to 68, wherein said gel, upon contacting said body of said subject, result in a skin-to-electrode electrical impedance of no more than 2 times higher than a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both, as measured by an Electrochemical Impedance Spectrometer (EIS) at a frequency of 0.1 Hz. 70. The method of any one of claims 63 to 69, wherein said gel, upon contacting said body of said subject, result in a skin-to-electrode impedance that is at least 2%, or at least 5%, or at least 10% lower than a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both. 71. The method of any one of claims 63 to 70, wherein said gel, upon contacting said body of said subject, result in a skin-to-electrode impedance that is sufficiently lower than a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both, by at least 5 times, at least 6 times, or at least 7 times. 72. The method of any one of claims 63 to 71, wherein said gel, upon contacting a body of said subject, conformably rest upon said flexible or deformable substrate. 73. The method of any one of claims 63 to 72, wherein said gel, upon contacting a body of said subject, transmit a sensed signal to said at least one electrode, optionally said at least one electrode is placed or applied on said flexible or deformable substrate; and wherein said subject is stationary or in motion. 74. The method of any one of claims 63 to 73, wherein the method reduces motion artifacts during said sensing by showing at least one more characteristic feature of a waveform compared to a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both. 75. The method of any one of claims 63 to 74, wherein the method reduces motion artifacts during said sensing by reducing at least 10% baseline noise compared to a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both. 76. The method of any one of claims 63 to 75, wherein the method improves accuracy of said sensing by at least 10% compared to a reference wet-electrode, or a reference dry electrode, or both. 77. The method of any one of claims 63 to 76, wherein said gel comprises a hydrogel derived from a mixture comprising by weight: about 10% to about 80% monomers; about 0.005% to about 0.5% a crosslinker; about 0.005% to about 5% an initiator; about 1% to about 20% an ionic salt; about 5% to about 50% a humectant; and about 5% to about 50% water. 78. The method of claim 77, wherein: said monomers comprise acrylamide (AAm) and dimethylacrylamide (DM A Am); said crosslinker comprises N,Nâ -methyl enebisacrylamide (bisacrylamide); said initiator comprises ammonium persulfate (APS) and tetramethylethylene diamine (TEMED); said ionic salt comprises poly(diallyldimethylammonium chloride) (PDADMAC); and said humectant comprises glycerine. 79. The method of claim 78, wherein said mixture comprises by weight: about 0.005% to about 0.010% APS, and about 1% to about 5% TEMED. 80. The method of any one of claims 76 to 79, wherein said gel further comprises an interpenetrating hydrophobic network. 81. The method of claim 80, wherein said interpenetrating hydrophobic network comprises polymerized tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS). 82. The method of claim 80 or 81, wherein said mixture further comprises by weight about 5% to about 20% of a hydrophobic network precursor. 83. The method of claim 82, wherein said hydrophobic network precursor comprises tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS).
